title: Missing Semicolon
beginnerExplanation: Your code finished a statement but forgot to put a semicolon (;) at the end. The compiler uses it to know the sentence is over.
likelyFixes: Add a `;` at the end of the highlighted line.
Look one line ABOVE the error — the missing ; is often there.
Check the last statement in that block, especially after return/if/let.
Save the file — TypeScript auto-inserts on save if format-on-save is on.

marathi: Bhau, semicolon suttivar gelay ka? ; tak na.
Ek chota viram chinh visarla ani compiler cha bawa zala.
Vada pav cha diet ulta, pan semicolon cha diet pudhe theva.
Bhau, shevati ; tak, compiler la full stop pahije.
Pudhchi line la thamba, ; bhulala ka?
Semicolon adhich code madhye javal, tyala sodun de.
Bagh, compiler la ek 'full stop' pahijech — ; chalel.
Bhau, itka sampurna code pan ek semicolon sathi radtoy.
Semicolon nasel tar compiler che don konad radnarach!
Thamb, ek cup chai ghe, ek ; tak, punha bagh.
hindi: Bhai, semicolon bhul gaya kya? ; laga de.
Ek chhota sa punctuation miss kiya, compiler pareshan.
Vada pav diet toh ulta, semicolon diet aage rakh.
Bhai, aakhir me ; laga, compiler ko full stop chahiye.
Aage wali line ko rukne ka mauka de, ; bhula nahi.
Semicolon pehle code mein aana chahiye, use chhod de.
Dekh, compiler ko ek 'full stop' chahiye — ; chalega.
Bhai, itna bada code aur ek semicolon ke liye ro raha hai.
Semicolon nahi toh compiler ke dono kone ronewale!
Ruk, ek cup chai le, ek ; laga, phir dekh.
english: Bhau, did the semicolon slip out of your pocket? Drop one in.
Compiler is hungry — feed it a ';'.
One tiny dot-comma and the whole parade stopped.
You wrote a thesis but forgot the period at the end.
Semicolon: the smallest guest your compiler refuses to start without.
Add a ';' — that's the price of admission.
Imagine ending a sentence mid-word. That's this line right now.
Bhau, the compiler is not a mind-reader. Give it the ';'.
It's one keystroke. One. Just one ';'.
Pause, sip chai, drop a ';' — done.

patterns: Module not found: Error: Can't resolve
title: Missing Package
beginnerExplanation: Your code is trying to use a package or file that is not installed in your project. Like inviting a guest who was never on the list.
likelyFixes: Run `npm install <package>` (or `pnpm`/`yarn` equivalent).
Check `package.json` — is the package listed under `dependencies`?
Restart your dev server / TS language server after installing.
Make sure you're running the install command in the correct folder.
For local files, check the relative path and file extension.

marathi: Bhau, party la bolavla pan guest aalach nahi.
Package install nahi kela, pan code madhye bolavla— bhau?
Na bolavlela guest, na aalela guest, pan npmnacha table ready aahe.
Node_modules madhye bagh, to package adhich sutla ka?
Bhau, package.json var bagh, konha package miss zala?
Import karaycha aadhi install kar, mag bhau ban.
Package koni import kela, pan npm la kalalach nahi— introduction garaj!
Guest list var nav, pan invitation pathvla— bhau, tyala import kela?
Npm install sodun code chalu kela, tar compiler na radnar ka?
Bhau, ek package sathi code chalu, ek sathi npm install— donhi kar.
hindi: Bhai, party mein bulaya par guest aaya hi nahi.
Package install nahi kiya, par code mein bula liya— bhai?
Na bulaya guest, na aaya guest, par npm ka table ready hai.
Node_modules mein dekh, woh package toh chhoot gaya kya?
Bhai, package.json par dekh, kaun sa package miss ho gaya.
Import karne se pehle install kar, phir bhai ban.
Package kisi ne import kiya, par npm ko hi nahi pata— introduction garaj!
Guest list par naam nahi, par invitation bhej diya— bhai, import kiya?
Npm install chhod code chalu kiya, toh compiler na roye kya?
Bhai, ek package ke liye code, ek ke liye npm install— dono kar.
english: Bhau, you invited someone to the party who was never on the guest list.
Your code shouted a name across the room — nobody answered.
You imported a hero, but the script never hired them.
Package: Not Installed. Code: Already Calling. Bhau: Confused.
Imagine calling a friend who isn't saved in your phone. That.
npm install first, friendship later.
The package exists in your import line, not in your node_modules. Fix that.
You can't marry someone you never met. Install them first.
Bhau, node_modules looks empty. Like a tiffin box without the vada.
First 'npm install', then 'import'. Always in that order, bhau.

title: Undefined Variable
beginnerExplanation: You used a variable name that you never declared. The compiler doesn't know what box you're talking about.
likelyFixes: Check the spelling — a typo turns `userName` into `usreName`.
Add a `let`, `const` or `var` declaration before using the variable.
Make sure the variable is declared in the same scope (not in another function).
If it's an import, check the import statement at the top of the file.
For globals (like `process` or `Buffer`), install the matching `@types/*` package.

marathi: Bhau, variable cha nav ghetla pan introduce nahi kela.
Compiler la hi nav kalat nahi— bhau, parichay karav lagel.
Nav likhun takla, pan tyala janun ghetla nahi— import kiva declare kar.
Bhau, hi variable tya room madhye nahi, dusrya room madhye aahe.
Nav visarla ka? Ekdam varkhad pan declar karun ghe.
Bhau, hi value kuthun ali? Compiler la pan mahiti nahi.
Variable declare nahi kela, pan vapsar kela— bhau, jodne aadhi ghatti kar.
Naam toh sahi, pan tyache kuthe aalo mahiti nahi— bhau?
Bhau, hi variable ghari nahi, dusrya function madhye aahe— import kar.
Compiler mhante: 'He nav kuthun aale?'— bhau, tyala sang.
hindi: Bhai, variable ka naam liya par introduce nahi kiya.
Compiler ko yeh naam nahi pata— bhai, parichay karao padega.
Naam likh diya, par jaana nahi— import ya declare kar.
Bhai, yeh variable is room mein nahi, dusre room mein hai.
Naam bhul gaya kya? Ek baar declare toh kar le.
Bhai, yeh value kahan se aayi? Compiler ko bhi nahi pata.
Variable declare nahi kiya, par use kar liya— bhai, pehle jod le.
Naam sahi hai, par kahan se aaya pata nahi— bhai?
Bhai, yeh variable ghar mein nahi, dusre function mein hai— import kar.
Compiler kahta: 'Yeh naam kahan se aaya?'— bhai, use bata.
english: Bhau, you used a name the compiler has never heard of.
Imagine calling a stranger by their first name at a wedding. Awkward.
You referenced a variable. You never declared it. Bhau.
The compiler is asking: 'Who? I don't know them.'
Variable not declared, but you used it like an old friend.
Bhau, spelling check kar — typo turns heroes into ghosts.
Declare first. Use later. That's the rule of the parade.
This name lives in another scope. You need to import it.
Bhau, the variable exists in your head, not in your code.
Compiler confusion = 100. Reason = undeclared name. Fix = declare it.

patterns: IndexError: list index out of range
TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined \(reading '(\d+)'\)
title: Array Out Of Bounds
beginnerExplanation: You tried to access an array element at an index that doesn't exist — usually because the array is shorter than you think, or you used `array.length` instead of `array.length - 1`.
likelyFixes: console.log the array's `.length` right before the failing line.
Use `array[array.length - 1]` for the last item, not `array[array.length]`.
Add a bounds check: `if (i < arr.length) { ... }`.
Use `.at(-1)` (modern JS) or `array[array.length - 1]` for the last item.
For `map`/`filter` chains: log intermediate arrays — one might be empty.

marathi: Bhau, hi index array chya baher ahe— length check kar.
List aiti item ghetla, pan listat charuch nahi— IndexError.
Bhau, array.length ahe, pan array.length-1 pahije— last item.
Index out of bounds— ek cup chai ghe, length console.log kar.
Bhau, hi array rikami aahe, pan tumhi item shodhta— kase?
Loop cha index 0 pasun chalu, length paryant— rule bagh.
Bhau, hi index negative ahe? Array madhye negative index nahi.
Array cha last item pahije? .at(-1) vapr, bhau.
Bhau, hi value undefined aahe, kyunki index chukla.
Array out of bounds— compiler la confusion nahi, tuze code la ahe.
hindi: Bhai, yeh index array ke bahar hai— length check kar.
List mein aathva item liya, par list mein chaar hi nahi— IndexError.
Bhai, array.length hai, par array.length-1 chahiye— last item.
Index out of bounds— ek cup chai le, length console.log kar.
Bhai, yeh array khali hai, par tum item dhoondhte ho— kaise?
Loop ka index 0 se shuru, length tak— rule dekh.
Bhai, yeh index negative hai? Array mein negative index nahi.
Array ka last item chahiye? .at(-1) use kar, bhai.
Bhai, yeh value undefined hai, kyunki index galat hai.
Array out of bounds— compiler ko confusion nahi, tumhare code ko hai.
english: Bhau, that index walked off the edge of the array.
You asked for item 8 in a 4-item array. Arrays don't stretch.
Bhau, the last index is `length - 1`. Not `length`. Never `length`.
console.log(arr.length) before the failing line. See the truth.
Bhau, empty array + index access = guaranteed undefined.
Loops start at 0, end at length - 1. That's the rule, bhau.
Negative index? Use `.at(-1)`. Don't subtract from length.
Bhau, if `arr[i]` is undefined, `i` is wrong. Check it.
Array bounds: where optimism meets reality.
Bhau, your index is bigger than your array. Math doesn't lie.

patterns: TypeError: Cannot read propert(?:y|ies) of undefined \(reading 'then'\)
title: Promise Rejection
beginnerExplanation: A Promise rejected but you didn't catch it, or you called `.then()` on something that isn't a Promise. Async code needs `await` + `try/catch` or `.then().catch()`.
likelyFixes: Wrap your `await` calls in `try/catch` blocks.
Always add `.catch()` to `.then()` chains.
Check that the function you're awaiting actually returns a Promise.
Add `async` to the function that uses `await`.
Listen for `process.on('unhandledRejection', ...)` to catch strays in Node.

marathi: Bhau, hi promise reject zali, pan tumhi catch nahi kela.
Await vaprta, pan function async nahi— bhau, fix kar.
Bhau, .then() var .catch() nahi— radnar, thev na.
Promise reject zala, pan koni handle nahi kela— bhau?
Async function la await nahi— control flow radtoy.
Bhau, try/catch vapr, mag promise reject pan chamtkar nahi.
Unhandled promise— Node console red karto, fix kar.
Bhau, hi value promise nahi, pan tumhi .then() karta— kase?
Promise chain adhich todi, pan error nahi pakaDla— bhau.
Async/await cha rule bagh— try/catch garaj, bhau.
hindi: Bhai, yeh promise reject hua, par tum catch nahi kiya.
Await use karte, par function async nahi— bhai, fix kar.
Bhai, .then() par .catch() nahi— roega, rakh na.
Promise reject hua, par kisi ne handle nahi kiya— bhai?
Async function ko await nahi— control flow ro raha hai.
Bhai, try/catch use kar, phir promise reject bhi chamatkar nahi.
Unhandled promise— Node console red karta hai, fix kar.
Bhai, yeh value promise nahi, par tum .then() karte ho— kaise?
Promise chain pehle todi, par error nahi pakda— bhai.
Async/await ka rule dekh— try/catch garaz, bhai.
english: Bhau, your promise rejected. Where's the catch?
Using `await` in a non-async function? Bhau, add `async`.
Every `.then()` deserves a `.catch()`. They're a couple, bhau.
Promise rejected silently. Now Node is screaming.
Bhau, async code without error handling is a ticking bomb.
Wrap awaits in try/catch. Your future self will thank you.
Bhau, unhandled rejections can crash Node 15+. Catch them.
Calling `.then()` on a non-Promise? It returns undefined. Bhau.
Bhau, promises are commitments. If they fail, you need a backup plan.
async/await + try/catch = the only correct pattern. Bhau, learn it.

patterns: Object reference not set to an instance of an object
Attempted to dereference a null object
title: Null Reference
beginnerExplanation: You tried to read a property or call a method on something that is `null` or `undefined` — like opening an empty tiffin box and expecting a vada.
likelyFixes: Use optional chaining: `user?.name` instead of `user.name`.
Add a null check before accessing: `if (user) { ... }`.
Initialize your variables / state with sensible defaults.
Console.log the variable just before the failing line — see what it actually is.
Check where the value comes from — an API? An empty array? A skipped render?

marathi: Bhau, rikami dabba ughadla, pan vada aalach nahi.
Null var bagh ahe, pan tyat kahich nahi— bhau, tuze code radtoy.
Rikami chair var basaycha, pan tyat koni nahi— TypeError!
Bhau, hi value null aahe, pan tumhi method call karta— kase?
Tiffin rikami, pan jevan shodhita— bhau, null check kar.
Compiler mhante: 'He khali aahe, pan tumhi var chadhta'— bhau?
Bhau, ek value null aahe, pan tumhi tyach var bagh ahe.
Rikami box madhye hath ghalta, pan tyat kaay mitla— null!
Bhau, null la method shikvaycha? Tyala logic nahi yet.
Optional chaining (?) vapr, mag null pan chamtkar nahi.
hindi: Bhai, khali dabba khola, par vada hi nahi.
Null par dekh raha, par usme kuch nahi— bhai, code ro raha hai.
Khali kursi par baithna, par usme koi nahi— TypeError!
Bhai, yeh value null hai, par tum method call karte ho— kaise?
Tiffin khali, par khana dhoondhta— bhai, null check kar.
Compiler kahta: 'Yeh khali hai, par tum upar chadhte ho'— bhai?
Bhai, ek value null hai, par tum usi par dekh rahe ho.
Khali box mein haath daalta, par usme kya mila— null!
Bhai, null ko method sikhana? Use logic nahi aata.
Optional chaining (?) use kar, phir null bhi chamatkar nahi.
english: Bhau, you opened an empty box and asked for the vada inside.
Reading a property of null is like calling a friend who doesn't exist.
Your variable is `null`. You're treating it like a king. Stop.
Null is empty. Empty has no methods. Bhau, basic physics.
Bhau, the chair is empty. Don't ask it to stand up.
Imagine calling `.eat()` on an empty tiffin. That's this code.
Bhau, the value isn't there yet. Wait, check, then access.
Use `?.` — the optional chaining superhero. It saves lives.
Null: the silent guest. Don't ask it to dance.
Bhau, null is null. It can't `.map()`. It can't `.toUpperCase()`. Nothing.

patterns: TS2322: Type .* is not assignable to type
Cannot assign to read only property
Cannot convert undefined or null to object
TS2367: This condition will always return
Property .* does not exist on type
title: Type Error
beginnerExplanation: You're using a value as if it were one type, but it's actually another — like trying to call `.toUpperCase()` on a number, or treat a single object as a list.
likelyFixes: Console.log the value AND its type right before the failing line.
Check the function signature — what type does it actually expect?
For arrays, make sure you're not passing a single item where a list is needed.
For functions, check the import — maybe it's not actually a function.
Add TypeScript types so the compiler catches this earlier next time.

marathi: Bhau, he number aahe, pan tumhi string var method call karta— kase?
Type misal ahe— vada pav cha plate idli sathi vaprta.
Bhau, hi value function nahi, pan tumhi call karta— typo ka?
Compiler mhante: 'He array nahi, pan tumhi .map vaprta'— bhau?
Type alag ahe, pan tumhi same disha madhye challa— bhau, check kar.
Bhau, ek method chya shape madhye dusrya shape chi value takta— misalign.
String la number sathi vaprta, pan tyacha data type radtoy.
Bhau, type mismatch ahe— compiler la confusion zala.
Rice sathi plate, pan tumhi tiffin madhye chapati shodhta— bhau, type check kar.
He function nahi, value aahe— bhau, parenthesis () kasha s utarle?
hindi: Bhai, yeh number hai, par tum string par method call karte ho— kaise?
Type misal hai— vada pav ki plate idli ke liye use karte ho.
Bhai, yeh value function nahi, par tum call karte ho— typo kya?
Compiler kahta: 'Yeh array nahi, par tum .map use karte ho'— bhai?
Type alag hai, par tum same disha mein chalte ho— bhai, check kar.
Bhai, ek method ke shape mein dusre shape ki value daalte ho— misalign.
String ko number ke liye use karte ho, par uska data type rota hai.
Bhai, type mismatch hai— compiler ko confusion ho gaya.
Rice ke liye plate, par tiffin mein chapati dhoondhte ho— bhai, type check kar.
Yeh function nahi, value hai— bhai, parenthesis () kaise utar gaye?
english: Bhau, you're calling a method on a value of the wrong type.
It's a number. You're using it like a string. Type clash.
Bhau, this is not a function. Stop calling it like one.
Compiler says: 'Not an array.' You say: `.map()` anyway. Why, bhau?
Type mismatch — like serving biryani in a tea cup.
Bhau, the value is shaped like a circle. You're pushing it into a square hole.
You expected a list. You got a single item. Bhau, check the type.
TypeError: the classic 'I thought this was X but it was Y' story.
Bhau, console.log(typeof x) before this line. Save your sanity.
Types don't match. Either change the value or change the expectation.

title: Syntax Error
beginnerExplanation: Your code has a grammar mistake — a missing bracket, comma, or a typo the parser can't make sense of. Like writing a Marathi sentence without any verbs.
likelyFixes: Match every `(` with `)`, `{` with `}`, `[` with `]`.
Check for missing commas in objects, arrays, and function arguments.
Look at the line BEFORE the reported error — the real mistake is often there.
Paste your code into a linter (ESLint/Prettier) to auto-format and spot issues.
Re-read the line slowly — sometimes the typo is one wrong character.

marathi: Bhau, he sentence chukyavar ahe— shabd kram bagh.
Ek bracket visarla, pan compiler aadhich radtoy.
Bhau, hi line chukyivar ahe— punctuation bagh.
Marathi sentence madhye kriyapad nahi, tar kase chalen? Tasach code.
Bhau, ek comma visarla, pan compiler cha bawa zala.
Bracket mismatch— khodkun bagh, ekadhik ( ahe ka?
Bhau, parser la pul aala nahi— toka check kar.
Hi line chukyivar ahe— compiler la aadhi pul nahi, mag rasta nahi.
Bhau, syntax mhantat, grammar chuklyavar— bagh, ek cup chai ghe.
Ek token visarla, pan compiler full screen red karto— bhau, fix kar.
hindi: Bhai, yeh sentence galat hai— shabd kram dekh.
Ek bracket bhul gaya, par compiler pehle hi ro raha hai.
Bhai, yeh line galat hai— punctuation dekh.
Hindi sentence mein kriya nahi, toh kaise chalega? Waisa hi code.
Bhai, ek comma bhul gaya, par compiler ka bawa ho gaya.
Bracket mismatch— dhyan se dekh, ek adhik ( hai kya?
Bhai, parser ko pul nahi mila— toka check kar.
Yeh line galat hai— compiler ko pehle pul nahi, phir rasta nahi.
Bhai, syntax kehte hain, grammar galat— dekh, ek cup chai le.
Ek token bhul gaya, par compiler full screen red karta hai— bhai, fix kar.
english: Bhau, your sentence has no verb. The compiler is confused.
A missing bracket, a missing comma, and the whole line cries.
Bhau, parser walks in, sees chaos, leaves. Fix the punctuation.
Grammar mistake — the parser is the strict Marathi teacher here.
One stray token and the parser files a complaint.
Bhau, count your brackets. Like counting your change.
The error points HERE. The bug is one line ABOVE. Always.
Bhau, English without verbs makes no sense. Same for code without commas.
Re-read the line. Slowly. Out loud if needed. The typo will jump out.
Syntax error = the compiler saying 'I don't understand your handwriting.'

patterns: Rendered more hooks than during the previous render
React Hook .* is called in a function that is neither a React function component nor a custom React Hook
React has detected a change in the order of Hooks
Hooks can only be called inside the body of a component
title: React Hook Violation
beginnerExplanation: You broke React's rules of hooks — usually by calling a hook inside an `if`, a loop, or a non-component function. Hooks must run in the same order every render.
likelyFixes: Move the hook ABOVE any `if` / `return` / loop — never inside it.
Make sure the function name starts with a capital letter and returns JSX.
Check that you only have ONE copy of React installed (npm ls react).
Don't call hooks from regular JS functions — only from components or custom hooks.
For early returns, compute all hooks BEFORE the return statement.

marathi: Bhau, hook la if madhye takla, pan React mhante 'nahi, nahi'.
Hooks cha kram badlala, React la dhyas aala— bhau, ekdam var tak.
Bhau, hook function madhye nahi, pan component madhye pahije— navi thev.
Conditional hook? React la haa savay nahi— bhau, var thev.
Bhau, hook cha kram change nahi honar— aadhi var, mag if.
React hook rule toda, tar React radnar— bhau, simple rule.
Hook loop madhye? Nahi, nahi— bhau, ekdam top la tak.
Bhau, hook function madhye call kela, pan to component pahije— rename kar.
Hooks order change zala, tar state change zala— bhau, stable thev.
Ek hook conditionally, tar React aadhich confused— bhau, var kar.
hindi: Bhai, hook ko if mein daala, par React kahta 'nahi, nahi'.
Hooks ka kram badla, React ko confusion hua— bhai, ekdam upar rakh.
Bhai, hook function mein nahi, par component mein chahiye— nayi jagah.
Conditional hook? React ko yeh aadat nahi— bhai, upar rakh.
Bhai, hook ka kram change nahi hoga— pehle upar, phir if.
React hook rule toda, toh React roega— bhai, simple rule.
Hook loop mein? Nahi, nahi— bhai, ekdam top par rakh.
Bhai, hook function mein call kiya, par woh component chahiye— rename kar.
Hooks order change hua, toh state change hua— bhai, stable rakh.
Ek hook conditionally, toh React pehle confused— bhai, upar kar.
english: Bhau, hooks don't bend. Don't put them inside `if` statements.
React wants hooks in the same order, every single render.
Bhau, hook inside a loop? React says no. Always no.
Conditional hook = unpredictable state. Move it up.
Top of the component. Always. That's the hook's home address.
Bhau, you called a hook from a regular function. React caught you.
Hooks are like prayers — same time, same place, every day.
Bhau, if you `return` early, compute hooks first. Always.
Rule of hooks: top of component, never inside conditions.
Bhau, React is strict about hook order. Like a Pune bus schedule.

patterns: Ensure that the hook is only called once
title: Infinite Loop
beginnerExplanation: Your loop never exits — it keeps running forever. In React, the most common cause is calling setState directly inside the render body.
likelyFixes: Add a clear exit condition to your while/for loop.
For React: never call setState inside render — move it into useEffect.
Make sure your loop variable actually changes between iterations.
Drop a console.log inside the loop — if it spams, you've found it.
For recursion: ensure your base case is actually reachable.

marathi: Bhau, hi loop chukun saadlyavar ahe— nighat nahi.
React madhye setState render madhye takla— bhau, infinite loop.
Bhau, hi loop aadhi start, mag kabhothe nahi— condition bagh.
While loop cha exit nahi— bhau, ek cup chai ghe, punha vichar.
Bhau, recursion la base case nahi— to kharcha hot rahil.
Maximum update depth— React cha rada zala, bhau.
Loop variable change nahi hot, pan tumhi chalat ahe— bhau?
Bhau, hi loop kabhothe nighat nahi— rahu de, mi thambnar nahi.
Console.log takla, tar screen full zala— bhau, infinite loop.
Bhau, hi loop radnar nahi, pan computer radnar— fix kar.
hindi: Bhai, yeh loop galti se infinite ho gaya— nikalta nahi.
React mein setState render mein daala— bhai, infinite loop.
Bhai, yeh loop pehle start, phir kabhi nahi— condition dekh.
While loop ka exit nahi— bhai, ek cup chai le, phir vichar.
Bhai, recursion ko base case nahi— toh kharcha hota rahega.
Maximum update depth— React ka rada ho gaya, bhai.
Loop variable change nahi hota, par tum chalte ho— bhai?
Bhai, yeh loop kabhi nikalta nahi— rehne de, main rukunga nahi.
Console.log daala, toh screen full ho gaya— bhai, infinite loop.
Bhai, yeh loop roega nahi, par computer roega— fix kar.
english: Bhau, your loop forgot to stop. It's been running since morning.
React state update inside render = infinite loop. Move it to useEffect.
Bhau, where's the exit door? The loop is asking.
while(true) with no break is like a train with no brakes.
Bhau, recursion without a base case is a black hole for memory.
Maximum update depth — React is begging you to stop.
If your loop variable doesn't change, the loop doesn't end. Bhau.
Bhau, console.log inside the loop. If it spams, you found the bug.
Infinite loop: where CPUs go to overheat.
Bhau, the loop has no exit strategy. Like a Pune traffic jam.

patterns: ERROR in.*error Command failed with exit code
title: Build Failure
beginnerExplanation: Your project failed to build. This is usually a downstream effect of a TypeScript error, missing import, or invalid syntax somewhere in the codebase.
likelyFixes: Read the FULL build log — the first error is usually the real one.
Run `npm run build` locally to reproduce before deploying.
Check for TypeScript errors with `npx tsc --noEmit`.
Make sure every import points to a file that actually exists.
Delete `node_modules` and `package-lock.json`, then `npm install` fresh.

marathi: Bhau, build fail zala— ekdam pahili error bagh.
Build radtoy, pan tumhi kuthlya error var bagh ahe?
Bhau, hi build nahi honar— adhich tsc check kar.
Build fail zala, pan console madhye javail laksh thev.
Bhau, npm cha rada zala— package.json var bagh.
Vercel var build fail zala, pan local var chalto— bhau, version mismatch?
Bhau, build madhye error ahe, pan aadhich import check kar.
node_modules delete kar, npm install kar— punha try.
Bhau, build radnar, pan tu thambu naka— fix kar, punha try.
Build fail— ek cup chai ghe, full log ekdam varkhun wagh.
hindi: Bhai, build fail ho gaya— ekdam pehli error dekh.
Build ro raha, par tum kise error par dekh rahe ho?
Bhai, yeh build nahi hogi— pehle tsc check kar.
Build fail ho gaya, par console mein dhyan rakh.
Bhai, npm ka rada ho gaya— package.json par dekh.
Vercel par build fail, par local par chalta— bhai, version mismatch?
Bhai, build mein error hai, par pehle import check kar.
node_modules delete kar, npm install kar— phir try kar.
Bhai, build roega, par tu rukna mat— fix kar, phir try kar.
Build fail— ek cup chai le, full log ekdam dhyan se dekh.
english: Bhau, build failed. Look at the FIRST error — that's the real bug.
Build is crying. Open the full log, not just the last line.
Bhau, run `npx tsc --noEmit` — TypeScript will tell you what's wrong.
Build failures are symptoms. The disease is somewhere above.
Bhau, delete node_modules, npm install, try again. The classic.
Local works, Vercel fails? Check your Node version, bhau.
Bhau, the build is a strict teacher. It won't compromise.
Build failed = the compiler found something you missed in the editor.
Bhau, read the error, don't just stare at it.
Build failures teach more than successes. Read, fix, repeat.

patterns: SyntaxError: Cannot use import statement outside a module
TS1192: Module .* has no exported member
does not provide an export named
Module parse failed: Cannot use import statement
ImportDeclaration must be at top level
title: Import Error
beginnerExplanation: You're trying to import something that doesn't exist in the source module, or you're using `import` in a non-module context.
likelyFixes: Check whether the named export actually exists: `export const foo` not `export default foo`.
For default exports, use `import foo from` not `import { foo } from`.
For CommonJS, use `require()` instead of `import`, or set `"type": "module"` in package.json.
Verify the file path and extension are correct.
If importing a type, use `import type { Foo }` so it gets stripped at build time.

marathi: Bhau, import kela pan export nahi— relation aadhi contract pahije.
Import error— source madhye hi value nahi, pan tumhi ghet ahe.
Bhau, named import default import sarkha vaprta— confusion.
Import madhye nav chukla, pan compiler aadhich radnar.
Bhau, ek file madhye nahi, dusryat aahe— path check kar.
CommonJS file madhye import? require vapr, mag chalnar.
Bhau, default export nahi, pan tumhi default import karta— fix.
Type import nahi, pan tumhi type sarkha ghet ahe— `import type` vapr.
Bhau, file extension chukli— .js ki .ts, ekdam bagh.
Import name chukla, pan compiler full screen red— bhau, ek cup chai.
hindi: Bhai, import kiya par export nahi— relation se pehle contract chahiye.
Import error— source mein yeh value nahi, par tum le rahe ho.
Bhai, named import default import jaisa use karte ho— confusion.
Import mein naam galat, par compiler pehle roega.
Bhai, ek file mein nahi, dusre mein hai— path check kar.
CommonJS file mein import? require use kar, phir chalega.
Bhai, default export nahi, par tum default import karte ho— fix.
Type import nahi, par tum type jaisa le rahe ho— `import type` use kar.
Bhai, file extension galat— .js ya .ts, ekdam dekh.
Import name galat, par compiler full screen red— bhai, ek cup chai.
english: Bhau, you imported a name that's not exported. The module doesn't have it.
Default export vs named import. They don't mix, bhau.
Bhau, the source file doesn't export what you're asking for.
Check the spelling. `Button` and `button` are different in JS.
Bhau, CommonJS file? Use `require`. ESM? Use `import`. They're not friends.
Type-only import? Use `import type { Foo }`. The compiler likes honesty.
Bhau, file extension matters. `.js` ≠ `.ts` ≠ `.jsx` ≠ `.tsx`.
Import paths are case-sensitive on Linux, bhau. Check your caps.
You imported `Foo`. The file exports `foo`. Bhau, that's a typo.
Bhau, read the export list. Match the import. It's a contract.

patterns: TS2308: Module .* has already exported a member named
Only one default export allowed per module
title: Export Error
beginnerExplanation: You're exporting the same name twice, or have two default exports in the same file. Each module can have only one default export.
likelyFixes: Search the file for the duplicate name — there can be only one.
Use named exports if you need multiple exports: `export const a; export const b;`.
Remove one of the duplicate `export` keywords.
For default exports, ensure there's exactly ONE per file.
Rename one of the conflicting exports.

marathi: Bhau, hi nav donda export kela— ekach nav, ekach aahe.
Don default export ek file madhye? Bhau, hi impossible aahe.
Bhau, hi nav duplicate aahe— rename kar, mag chalnar.
Ek file, ek default export— hi rule aahe, bhau.
Bhau, export conflict— compiler la bawa zala, fix kar.
Don var same nav, pan ek export— bhau, rename kar.
Duplicate declaration— bhau, file var var bagh.
Bhau, ek nav, ek export— simple rule, bhau.
Don default export— hi nahi chalnar, ek thev.
Bhau, export error— file madhye duplicate nav bagh.
hindi: Bhai, yeh naam dono taraf export kiya— ek hi naam, ek hi hai.
Do default export ek file mein? Bhai, yeh impossible hai.
Bhai, yeh naam duplicate hai— rename kar, phir chalega.
Ek file, ek default export— yeh rule hai, bhai.
Bhai, export conflict— compiler ka bawa ho gaya, fix kar.
Do taraf same naam, par ek export— bhai, rename kar.
Duplicate declaration— bhai, file par file dekh.
Bhai, ek naam, ek export— simple rule, bhai.
Do default export— yeh nahi chalega, ek rakh.
Bhai, export error— file mein duplicate naam dekh.
english: Bhau, you exported the same name twice. Pick one.
Two default exports in one file? That's not how modules work, bhau.
Bhau, rename one of them — duplicate names break the contract.
One file, one default export. That's the rule.
Bhau, the compiler is strict about duplicate exports.
You can't have two things with the same name leaving the same door.
Bhau, check the whole file. The duplicate is hiding somewhere.
Duplicate export = two keys for the same lock. Doesn't work.
Bhau, remove the duplicate. Or rename it. Choose one.
Export conflict — the compiler is calling you out, bhau.

title: Missing Dependency
beginnerExplanation: npm can't figure out which versions of packages to install because two packages want incompatible versions of the same dependency.
likelyFixes: Read the conflict — npm usually names the two packages fighting.
Try installing with `--legacy-peer-deps` as a temporary workaround.
Update one of the conflicting packages to a compatible version.
Delete `node_modules` and `package-lock.json`, then reinstall fresh.
Check the `peerDependencies` of your top-level packages.

marathi: Bhau, don package ekach dependency sathi ladhtaat— fix kar.
npm cha ERESOLVE— version conflict aahe, bhau.
Bhau, peer dependency problem— package version bagh.
Don package ek version sathi ladhle— bhau, ek update kar.
npm install radtoy— bhau, package.json var bagh.
Bhau, --legacy-peer-deps try kar, pan permanently fix kar.
node_modules delete kar, punha install kar— fresh start.
Bhau, peer dependency conflict— version match kar.
npm la conflict ahe— bhau, donhi package check kar.
Bhau, dependency tree radtoy— ek package update kar.
hindi: Bhai, do package ekhi dependency ke liye ladte hain— fix kar.
npm ka ERESOLVE— version conflict hai, bhai.
Bhai, peer dependency problem— package version dekh.
Do package ek version ke liye ladte— bhai, ek update kar.
npm install rota hai— bhai, package.json par dekh.
Bhai, --legacy-peer-deps try kar, par permanently fix kar.
node_modules delete kar, phir install kar— fresh start.
Bhai, peer dependency conflict— version match kar.
npm ko conflict hai— bhai, dono package check kar.
Bhai, dependency tree ro raha hai— ek package update kar.
english: Bhau, two packages want different versions of the same thing.
npm ERESOLVE — the package manager is having a family feud.
Bhau, peer dependency conflict. Read which two packages are fighting.
Try `--legacy-peer-deps` as a bandage, but fix the root cause.
Bhau, update one of the conflicting packages to a friendlier version.
Sometimes nuking `node_modules` and starting fresh is the only way.
Bhau, check `peerDependencies` in package.json — that's where it lives.
Dependency conflicts are like roommate fights. Someone has to compromise.
Bhau, npm is strict for a reason. Don't bypass it forever.
Read the conflict, identify the two fighters, pick a winner.

patterns: NetworkError when attempting to fetch resource
title: API Fetch Failure
beginnerExplanation: Your code tried to fetch data from an API but couldn't — either the URL is wrong, the server is down, or CORS is blocking the request from the browser.
likelyFixes: Open the URL directly in your browser — does it return anything?
Check for typos in the URL and the protocol (http vs https).
If CORS error, the BACKEND needs to send `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` header.
Verify the server is running (for local APIs: is your backend on port 3000?).
Try adding or removing a trailing `/` from the URL.

marathi: Bhau, fetch fail zala— URL bagh, server bagh.
API la pillu nahi— URL chukli ka, server down ka?
Bhau, CORS error aahe— backend la header thevaycha ahe, frontend nahi.
Fetch radtoy— URL madhye protocol check kar (http/https).
Bhau, server alive aahe ka? Console madhye bagh.
Localhost fetch nahi hot— backend port check kar, bhau.
Bhau, fetch fail— ek cup chai ghe, URL punha check kar.
Status 404— server la hi endpoint mahiti nahi, bhau.
Bhau, fetch error— network tab var bagh, request pahata.
Fetch radnar, pan URL, port, CORS— donhi check kar.
hindi: Bhai, fetch fail ho gaya— URL dekh, server dekh.
API ko pillu nahi— URL galat kya, server down kya?
Bhai, CORS error hai— backend ko header dena hai, frontend ko nahi.
Fetch ro raha hai— URL mein protocol check kar (http/https).
Bhai, server alive hai kya? Console mein dekh.
Localhost fetch nahi hota— backend port check kar, bhai.
Bhai, fetch fail— ek cup chai le, URL phir check kar.
Status 404— server ko yeh endpoint nahi pata, bhai.
Bhai, fetch error— network tab par dekh, request dekho.
Fetch roega, par URL, port, CORS— dono check kar.
english: Bhau, the fetch failed. Check the URL, then the server.
APIs don't answer to typos. Check your URL, bhau.
Bhau, CORS error means the BACKEND needs a header, not your frontend.
Fetch failed — is the server even running? Check the console.
Bhau, http vs https. One letter, big difference.
Localhost not responding? Your backend is probably on a different port.
Bhau, 404 means the server doesn't know that URL. Check the route.
Network tab → see the request → see the response. The truth is there.
Bhau, fetch errors are like missed calls. Was the phone even on?
Fetch failed: URL, port, CORS, auth. Check all four, bhau.

patterns: Unexpected token .* in JSON at position
title: JSON Parse Error
beginnerExplanation: You're trying to parse a string as JSON, but it's not valid JSON — usually a missing quote, a trailing comma, or HTML being returned instead of JSON.
likelyFixes: console.log the raw string before parsing — see what's actually there.
Remove trailing commas (JSON does not allow them).
Use double quotes for all keys and string values, not single quotes.
If your API returned HTML (like a 404 page), don't parse it as JSON.
Paste the JSON into jsonlint.com to spot the exact error position.

marathi: Bhau, hi JSON nahi— ek quote visarla ka?
JSON parse error— trailing comma nahi chalat, bhau.
Bhau, single quote nahi, double quote pahije JSON madhye.
API ne HTML pathvla, pan tumhi JSON parse karta— bhau?
Bhau, ek comma extra— pan parser full red karto.
JSON parse error— console.log kar, raw string bagh.
Bhau, hi JSON invalid aahe— ek validator vapar.
Unexpected token— ek quote, comma, bracket bagh.
Bhau, JSON la strict grammar— single quote nahi.
JSON parse radtoy— file madhye bagh, raw text.
hindi: Bhai, yeh JSON nahi— ek quote bhul gaya kya?
JSON parse error— trailing comma nahi chalega, bhai.
Bhai, single quote nahi, double quote chahiye JSON mein.
API ne HTML bheja, par tum JSON parse karte ho— bhai?
Bhai, ek comma extra— par parser full red karta hai.
JSON parse error— console.log kar, raw string dekh.
Bhai, yeh JSON invalid hai— ek validator use kar.
Unexpected token— ek quote, comma, bracket dekh.
Bhai, JSON ko strict grammar— single quote nahi.
JSON parse ro raha hai— file mein dekh, raw text.
english: Bhau, that's not JSON. There's a quote missing somewhere.
JSON doesn't allow trailing commas. Remove the last one, bhau.
Bhau, JSON uses double quotes only. Single quotes are for JS objects.
API returned HTML, you tried to JSON.parse it. Bhau, check status first.
Bhau, one extra comma breaks the whole parser. Be ruthless.
console.log the raw string before parsing. See what's actually there.
Bhau, use a JSON validator. It'll point to the exact character.
Unexpected token usually = a missing comma, quote, or bracket.
Bhau, JSON is strict. JavaScript objects are flexible. They're different.
JSON parse error: the data isn't shaped the way you think it is.

patterns: error: failed to push some refs
Updates were rejected because the remote contains work
title: Git Merge Conflict
beginnerExplanation: Two branches changed the same lines of code, and Git doesn't know which version to keep. You have to manually choose — `<<<<<<<`, `=======`, `>>>>>>>` mark the conflicting sections.
likelyFixes: Open each conflicted file and look for `<<<<<<< HEAD` markers.
Pick one version (or merge both), then DELETE the marker lines.
Stage the resolved file: `git add <file>`.
Commit the merge: `git commit` (Git pre-fills the message).
Use `git merge --abort` to bail out and start over if it's too messy.

marathi: Bhau, don branches ekach line var takle— conflict zala.
Git la confusion zala— tumhi decide Kara ki konta version sahi.
Bhau, <<<<<<< HEAD markers bagh, ekach fix kar.
Merge conflict— ek cup chai ghe, file var file bagh.
Bhau, ek version choose kar, markers delete kar, commit kar.
Conflict zala, pan git merge --abort kar— fresh start.
Bhau, don devs ne ekach line edit keli— conflict inevitable.
Conflict markers: <<<<<<< ======= >>>>>>> — bagh, fix kar.
Bhau, merge conflict— panic nako, ek file var laksh thev.
Resolve conflict— stage, commit, push. Simple 3 steps, bhau.
hindi: Bhai, do branches ne ek hi line par edit kiya— conflict hua.
Git ko confusion hua— tum decide karo kaunsa version sahi.
Bhai, <<<<<<< HEAD markers dekh, ek fix kar.
Merge conflict— ek cup chai le, file par file dekh.
Bhai, ek version choose kar, markers delete kar, commit kar.
Conflict hua, par git merge --abort kar— fresh start.
Bhai, do devs ne ek hi line edit ki— conflict inevitable.
Conflict markers: <<<<<<< ======= >>>>>>> — dekh, fix kar.
Bhai, merge conflict— panic mat, ek file par dhyan rakh.
Resolve conflict— stage, commit, push. Simple 3 steps, bhai.
english: Bhau, two branches fought over the same line. Pick a winner.
Git is confused. You have to choose: this version, that version, or both.
Bhau, look for `<<<<<<< HEAD` markers — those are the battlefields.
Merge conflict: panic optional. Chai mandatory. Then resolve file by file.
Pick one version, delete the markers, `git add`, `git commit`. Done.
Bhau, `git merge --abort` is your escape hatch. Use it if needed.
Two devs, same line, same time. Conflict was destiny.
Markers: `<<<<<<< ======= >>>>>>>`. They mark what to merge.
Bhau, resolve one file at a time. Don't panic-browse them all.
Stage → commit → push. The 3-step merge-conflict recovery.

patterns: listen EADDRINUSE: address already in use
Port .* is being used by another process
title: Port Already In Use
beginnerExplanation: Another process is already listening on the same port your app wants to use. Often it's a previous instance of your own server that didn't shut down.
likelyFixes: Find what's using the port: `lsof -i :3000` (mac/Linux) or `netstat -ano | findstr :3000` (Windows).
Kill the process: `kill -9 <PID>` (mac/Linux) or `taskkill /F /PID <PID>` (Windows).
Change your app's PORT env variable to a different number (e.g. 3001).
Make sure you didn't already start the server in another terminal.
On macOS: `sudo lsof -i :<port>` if you don't see the process.

marathi: Bhau, hi port aadhiIt occupied aahe— dusra process ahe.
EADDRINUSE— ek process aadhi chalu, pan tumhi navin start karta.
Bhau, lsof -i :3000 kar— kon ahe tithe bagh.
Port already in use— process kill kar, mag punha start.
Bhau, dusra terminal madhye server chalu aahe— band kar.
EADDRINUSE— ek cup chai ghe, process list bagh.
Bhau, hi port aadhi occupied— dusri port try kar.
Process kill kar: kill -9 <PID>— simple fix, bhau.
Bhau, port conflict— don servers ekach port var nahi chalat.
EADDRINUSE— env PORT change kar, mag chalnar.
hindi: Bhai, yeh port pehle occupied hai— dusra process hai.
EADDRINUSE— ek process pehle chalu, par tum naya start karte ho.
Bhai, lsof -i :3000 kar— kaun hai wahan dekh.
Port already in use— process kill kar, phir start kar.
Bhai, dusre terminal mein server chalu hai— band kar.
EADDRINUSE— ek cup chai le, process list dekh.
Bhai, yeh port pehle occupied— dusri port try kar.
Process kill kar: kill -9 <PID>— simple fix, bhai.
Bhai, port conflict— do servers ek hi port par nahi chalenge.
EADDRINUSE— env PORT change kar, phir chalega.
english: Bhau, that port is taken. Someone's sitting in your chair.
EADDRINUSE — there's already a server on that port. Find it.
Run `lsof -i :3000` (or your port). See who's squatting.
Bhau, `kill -9 <PID>` is your eviction notice.
Another terminal is probably still running your server. Close it.
Bhau, change the PORT env var to 3001. Move chairs.
Port conflicts are like reserved seats. You need a new one.
Bhau, two servers can't share a port. Basic math.
Windows? `netstat -ano | findstr :PORT` then `taskkill /F /PID`.
EADDRINUSE: classic 'forgot to close the previous server' bug.

title: Environment Variable Missing
beginnerExplanation: Your code is reading `process.env.SOMETHING` but the variable isn't set in your `.env` file or your environment. Often you forgot to create `.env` or restart the dev server after editing it.
likelyFixes: Create a `.env` file in the project root with the missing variable.
Restart your dev server — `.env` changes are not picked up automatically.
Check for typos: `API_KEY` ≠ `APIKEY` ≠ `api_key`.
In Vite/Next.js, prefix client-side env vars with `VITE_` or `NEXT_PUBLIC_`.
Never commit `.env` — but DO commit `.env.example` with the key names.

marathi: Bhau, process.env.SOMETHING undefined aahe— .env file bagh.
Env variable missing— .env file create kar, value thev.
Bhau, dev server restart kar— .env change nahi yet automatically.
Typo? API_KEY, APIKEY, api_key— ekdam bagh.
Bhau, Vite la VITE_ prefix pahije— rule aahe.
Env variable missing— ek cup chai ghe, .env file bagh.
Bhau, .env file git la commit nahi— pan .env.example commit kar.
process.env.X undefined— .env file var bagh, bhau.
Bhau, env variable chukla— case sensitive, dhyan thev.
Env missing— server restart kar, mag check kar.
hindi: Bhai, process.env.SOMETHING undefined hai— .env file dekh.
Env variable missing— .env file create kar, value rakh.
Bhai, dev server restart kar— .env change nahi hota automatically.
Typo? API_KEY, APIKEY, api_key— ekdam dekh.
Bhai, Vite ko VITE_ prefix chahiye— rule hai.
Env variable missing— ek cup chai le, .env file dekh.
Bhai, .env file git ko commit nahi— par .env.example commit kar.
process.env.X undefined— .env file par dekh, bhai.
Bhai, env variable galat— case sensitive, dhyan rakh.
Env missing— server restart kar, phir check kar.
english: Bhau, process.env.X is undefined. Check your .env file.
Env var missing — create `.env` and put the value in it.
Bhau, restart your dev server after editing `.env`. Always.
Case matters: `API_KEY` ≠ `api_key`. Bhau, check spelling.
Vite needs `VITE_` prefix. Next.js needs `NEXT_PUBLIC_`. Bhau, prefixes.
Bhau, `.env` is git-ignored. But `.env.example` should be committed.
process.env.X is undefined. Either X isn't set, or it's misspelled.
Bhau, dotenv in Node? Make sure you call `dotenv.config()` first.
Env var missing: classic 'works on my machine' bug source.
Bhau, check `.env.local` too — some frameworks override `.env` with it.

title: Database Connection Failure
beginnerExplanation: Your app couldn't connect to the database — either the DB isn't running, the URL is wrong, or the credentials are incorrect.
likelyFixes: Is your database server actually running? Check `docker ps` or services.
Verify the connection string: host, port, db name, username, password.
Check firewalls — is the DB port (5432, 27017, 3306) open from your machine?
For local dev: make sure Postgres/Mongo/MySQL is installed and started.
Test the connection from the terminal first: `psql`, `mongo`, `mysql`.

marathi: Bhau, database connect nahi zala— server chalu aahe ka?
ECONNREFUSED— DB port var koni nahi— server start kar.
Bhau, connection string check kar— host, port, password.
DB connection fail— credentials check kar, bhau.
Bhau, Postgres/Mongo chalu aahe ka? docker ps kar, bagh.
DB connection radtoy— firewall port block ka?
Bhau, password authentication fail— .env madhye DB password check.
Connection terminated— DB server gela, punha start kar.
Bhau, local DB install aahe ka? Psql/mongo/mysql terminal var try.
DB connection fail— ek cup chai ghe, server status bagh.
hindi: Bhai, database connect nahi hua— server chalu hai kya?
ECONNREFUSED— DB port par koi nahi— server start kar.
Bhai, connection string check kar— host, port, password.
DB connection fail— credentials check kar, bhai.
Bhai, Postgres/Mongo chalu hai kya? docker ps kar, dekh.
DB connection ro raha hai— firewall port block kya?
Bhai, password authentication fail— .env mein DB password check.
Connection terminated— DB server gaya, phir start kar.
Bhai, local DB install hai kya? Psql/mongo/mysql terminal par try.
DB connection fail— ek cup chai le, server status dekh.
english: Bhau, the database didn't pick up. Is the server even running?
ECONNREFUSED on the DB port — nothing's listening there.
Bhau, check the connection string. Host, port, db, user, password.
DB connection failed — credentials are the usual suspect.
Bhau, `docker ps` or `brew services list`. Is the DB container up?
Firewall blocking the DB port? Bhau, check security groups.
Password auth failed. `.env` DB password — check it letter by letter.
Bhau, connection terminated = DB server crashed. Restart it.
Test from terminal first: `psql`, `mongo`, `mysql`. If that fails, code won't help.
DB connection fail: server status → credentials → network. Check all three.
